goal_title_suggestions: Trade My Plan Every Single Day
Build Iron Discipline in My Trading

variants: Trade journal. Open a blank doc and write 'My Trading Rules' at the top.
Trade journal. Create a new doc called 'Trade Log' and save it.
Trade journal. Open a spreadsheet and label column headers: Date, Ticker, Entry, Exit, Result.
tasks.why: One place to track everything is the foundation of discipline.

variants: Your setup. Write one sentence describing the chart pattern you trade most.
Your setup. Write what price action must look like before you click buy.
Your setup. Write the one trade condition you look for every single morning.
tasks.why: Writing your setup makes it a rule, not a feeling.

variants: Exit rule. Write the exact price level where you take your stop loss.
Exit rule. Write the max dollar loss you will allow on one trade today.
Exit rule. Write one sentence for when you get out — no exceptions.
tasks.why: One clear exit rule stops you from holding losers too long.

variants: Trade audit. Open your broker and list your last 10 trades — win or loss.
Trade audit. Pull up closed trades and count how many were winners last week.
Trade audit. Write the ticker, entry price, and exit price for your last 5 trades.
tasks.why: You can only fix what you can see.

variants: Win rate. Divide your winners by total trades and write the percentage.
Win rate. Count winners vs. losers from your last 10 trades and write the ratio.
Win rate. Write your win count and loss count side by side from last week.
tasks.why: Knowing your baseline win rate is step one.

variants: Biggest loss. Find your worst trade and write what went wrong in one sentence.
Biggest loss. Look at your largest losing trade and write why you stayed in too long.
Biggest loss. Identify your costliest trade this month and write one thing you'd do differently.
tasks.why: Your worst trade always has a lesson.

variants: Profit/loss. Add up your total profit and total loss from last week's trades.
Profit/loss. Write your net P&L for each of the last 5 trading days.
Profit/loss. Calculate your average win size and average loss size from last week.
tasks.why: Seeing your real numbers removes blind spots.

variants: Risk ratio. Divide your average win by your average loss and write the number.
Risk ratio. Write whether your wins are bigger or smaller than your losses on average.
Risk ratio. Check if you make more per winner than you lose per loser — write yes or no.
tasks.why: A good risk/reward ratio is what makes trading math work.

variants: One fix. Write one change to your entries that could improve your win rate.
One fix. Write one rule you broke last week that cost you money.
One fix. Write the one thing you did wrong most often in your recent trades.
tasks.why: One focused fix beats ten vague improvements.

variants: Setup checklist. Write 3 things that must be true before you enter any trade.
Setup checklist. List the price action signals you need to see before clicking buy.
Setup checklist. Write your entry criteria as 3 short bullet points.
tasks.why: A checklist stops you from chasing random trades.

variants: Time filter. Write the specific hours when your setup works best.
Time filter. Write the market hours when you will and won't trade today.
Time filter. Write down the one or two time windows where you take trades.
tasks.why: Most edges only work in specific market hours.

variants: Stop rule. Write the exact dollar amount you'll stop trading after losing today.
Stop rule. Write your daily max loss — the number where you close your platform.
Stop rule. Write 'I stop trading today if I lose $___' and fill in the blank.
tasks.why: A hard stop loss protects you on your worst days.

variants: Rules review. Read your trading rules out loud from top to bottom.
Rules review. Read every rule you've written so far and cross out any that are vague.
Rules review. Read your rules and rewrite any sentence you wouldn't understand at 6am.
tasks.why: Rules only work if you know them cold.

variants: Missed trades. Look at today's charts and find one setup you should have taken.
Missed trades. Scroll back on a chart and mark one clean entry you skipped.
Missed trades. Find one trade from this week where the setup was there but you didn't act.
tasks.why: Spotting missed setups trains your eye faster than reading.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for how well you followed your rules today.
Day score. Rate today's discipline from 1 to 5 and write one reason why.
Day score. Give yourself a grade on rule-following today and write it down.
tasks.why: A daily score makes accountability concrete.

variants: Bad trades. Find your two worst trades this week and write the ticker and loss amount.
Bad trades. List the last three trades where you broke a rule.
Bad trades. Write down every trade this week where you entered without a clear setup.
tasks.why: Naming bad trades is the first step to stopping them.

variants: Pattern check. Write the one mistake that shows up most in your bad trades.
Pattern check. Do your losses happen at the same time of day? Write yes or no.
Pattern check. Write one thing your losing trades have in common.
tasks.why: Most traders have one recurring mistake hiding in plain sight.

variants: Fix rule. Write one new rule to prevent your most common mistake.
Fix rule. Add one sentence to your rules doc that blocks your worst habit.
Fix rule. Write 'I will NOT ___ because it costs me money' and fill in the blank.
tasks.why: Turning a mistake into a rule stops the bleeding.

variants: Week review. Write your total win count and loss count for the week.
Week review. Write your net P&L for the full week in one number.
Week review. List every trade from this week with a W or L next to each.
tasks.why: A weekly snapshot shows progress the daily view misses.

variants: Best trade. Write what went right on your top trade this week.
Best trade. Describe your best execution this week in one sentence.
Best trade. Write the ticker and what the setup looked like for your best trade.
tasks.why: Learning from wins is as important as learning from losses.

variants: Next week goal. Write one specific habit you want to improve next week.
Next week goal. Write one rule you will follow every single trade next week.
Next week goal. Write the one thing that would make next week better than this week.
tasks.why: A single weekly goal beats a list of five.

variants: Ticker list. Write the 3 tickers you trade most often.
Ticker list. List every symbol you traded last week.
Ticker list. Write which stocks or futures you watch every morning.
tasks.why: Trading fewer tickers builds deeper pattern recognition.

variants: Best ticker. Circle the one ticker where your win rate is highest.
Best ticker. Write the one symbol where you feel most confident in your reads.
Best ticker. Write the ticker that has made you the most money overall.
tasks.why: Specializing in one ticker sharpens your edge fast.

variants: Cut list. Write any ticker you traded last week that you should stop watching.
Cut list. Remove one symbol from your watchlist that distracts more than it earns.
Cut list. Write the one ticker where your results are worst and cross it off.
tasks.why: Removing distractions is as powerful as adding skill.

variants: Position size. Write how many shares or contracts you trade per setup.
Position size. Write your standard size and the max size you ever go.
Position size. Write 'My default trade size is ___' and fill it in.
tasks.why: Consistent size is the backbone of consistent results.

variants: Size rule. Write the rule for when you trade full size vs. half size.
Size rule. Write the condition that makes you reduce your position size.
Size rule. Write 'I trade smaller when ___' and fill in the blank.
tasks.why: Sizing down in bad conditions protects your capital.

variants: Over-trading. Count how many trades you took yesterday and write the number.
Over-trading. Write your ideal max number of trades per day.
Over-trading. Write whether you traded more or fewer setups than your plan allows yesterday.
tasks.why: Over-trading is the single fastest way to drain an account.

variants: Emotion log. Write how you felt before your last big losing trade.
Emotion log. Write whether you felt rushed, bored, or calm before your last entry.
Emotion log. Write the emotion word that describes how you trade when you're losing.
tasks.why: Emotions show up in trades before you notice them.

variants: Trigger words. Write two feelings that cause you to break your rules.
Trigger words. Write 'I break rules when I feel ___' and list two feelings.
Trigger words. Write the mental state you are in right before a revenge trade.
tasks.why: Naming your triggers is the first step to controlling them.

variants: Reset habit. Write one action you'll take after a loss to reset your focus.
Reset habit. Write what you will do for 60 seconds after a losing trade.
Reset habit. Write the one thing you'll do before you take your next trade after a loss.
tasks.why: A reset routine breaks the revenge-trade cycle.

variants: Chart review. Pull up yesterday's chart and mark every valid setup — whether you took it or not.
Chart review. Look at this morning's price action and circle every entry signal on the chart.
Chart review. Open a chart from last week and mark 3 spots where your setup appeared.
tasks.why: Marking charts builds pattern recognition faster than anything else.

variants: Entry quality. Write whether each trade you took today matched your entry rules exactly.
Entry quality. Look at your last 5 entries and write Y or N next to each — did it match your rules?
Entry quality. Write the one entry from this week that best matched your written rules.
tasks.why: Grading your entries reveals how often you actually follow your plan.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for how well you followed your entry rules today.
Day score. Rate your rule discipline today and write one thing you did well.
Day score. Score yourself 1–5 on entries today and write one thing to fix tomorrow.
tasks.why: Tracking your score daily creates a feedback loop.

variants: Stop loss review. Check your last 10 trades and write how many times you moved your stop.
Stop loss review. Write whether you honored your stop on every trade this week — yes or no.
Stop loss review. List the trades where you moved your stop loss and what it cost you.
tasks.why: Moving stops is the single most destructive habit in trading.

variants: Stop rule. Rewrite your stop loss rule in one sentence — no wiggle room.
Stop rule. Write 'My stop is at ___ and I will not move it' and fill in the blank.
Stop rule. Write the rule for where your stop goes the moment you enter a trade.
tasks.why: A clear stop rule written down is easier to follow.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for how strictly you followed your stop rules today.
Day score. Rate your stop discipline today and write yes or no on each trade.
Day score. Write whether you moved any stops today and what it cost or saved you.
tasks.why: Measuring stop discipline every day builds the habit.

variants: Full rules. Read every rule you've written over the last 12 days from top to bottom.
Full rules. Print or open your rules doc and read it out loud end to end.
Full rules. Read your complete rules list and check off each one you actually followed this week.
tasks.why: Reading your rules before trading is the cheapest edge you have.

variants: Rules audit. Cross out any rule that is vague or that you never follow.
Rules audit. Rewrite any rule that uses words like 'maybe' or 'sometimes'.
Rules audit. Cut your rules list down to the 5 most important and write them separately.
tasks.why: Fewer, clearer rules are easier to follow under pressure.

variants: Commit. Write 'I will follow rule #___ every single trade this week' and fill in the blank.
Commit. Choose your hardest rule to follow and write it at the top of your journal.
Commit. Write the one rule that would improve your results most if you followed it perfectly.
tasks.why: A single weekly commitment is more powerful than a vague intention.

variants: Two-week audit. Write your total trade count, win rate, and net P&L for days 1–14.
Two-week audit. List your 3 best trades and 3 worst trades from the past two weeks.
Two-week audit. Write your average win size and average loss size from the last 14 days.
tasks.why: A two-week snapshot shows whether your rules are working.

variants: Rule score. Give yourself a 1–5 rating for how consistently you followed your rules these two weeks.
Rule score. Write how many days out of 14 you followed all your rules.
Rule score. Write the three rules you broke most often in the last two weeks.
tasks.why: Grading yourself honestly is how you improve.

variants: Phase 2 goal. Write the one habit you will lock in for the next 30 days of daily journaling.
Phase 2 goal. Write what 'good execution' looks like for you going into next week.
Phase 2 goal. Write the number you want your win rate to be by the end of day 45.
tasks.why: Setting a clear phase goal before you start it doubles your follow-through.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write 1 setup you'll look for when the market opens today.
Pre-market plan. Write the ticker you'll watch and the price level that triggers your entry.
Pre-market plan. Write the one trade condition you need to see before you take any trade today.
tasks.why: A written pre-market plan cuts impulsive trades by half.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trade — ticker, entry price, exit price, win or loss.
Trade log. After your first trade, write the ticker and the result in your journal.
Trade log. Log every trade from today with entry, exit, and whether it matched your rules.
tasks.why: Logging trades in real time makes the data accurate.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 on how well you followed your rules today and one reason why.
Day score. Rate today's discipline 1–5 and write the best thing you did.
Day score. Score today 1–5 and write one thing you'll do differently tomorrow.
tasks.why: A daily score creates a 30-day improvement record.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the ticker, direction (long/short), and trigger price for today.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I will only trade if I see ___' and fill in the setup.
Pre-market plan. Write your trade idea for today in two sentences max.
tasks.why: Committing to a plan before the open protects you from noise.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades — ticker, entry, exit, result.
Trade log. Log each trade with a Y or N for whether it matched your setup rules.
Trade log. Write the number of trades you took today and the net result.
tasks.why: Consistent logging reveals patterns you can't see trade by trade.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for rule discipline today and one thing you did well.
Day score. Rate how closely you traded your plan today — 1 being not at all, 5 being perfect.
Day score. Write today's score and whether it's higher or lower than yesterday.
tasks.why: Tracking your score daily builds accountability.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the specific price level you need to see before entering today.
Pre-market plan. Write one bullish and one bearish scenario for your main ticker today.
Pre-market plan. Write your trade idea and the one thing that would cancel it.
tasks.why: Planning both scenarios keeps you flexible without being reactive.

variants: Trade log. Log today's trades with entry, exit, and rule compliance — Y or N.
Trade log. Write your P&L from today's session and total trade count.
Trade log. Write each trade and note whether you entered at the planned level.
tasks.why: Logging whether you hit your planned level is the key metric.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for today and the single best execution you made.
Day score. Score today's discipline and write whether you took any unplanned trades.
Day score. Write 1–5 and note if your score is trending up, down, or flat this week.
tasks.why: Noticing trends in your score is how you catch slippage early.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the market condition today — trending, choppy, or news-driven.
Pre-market plan. Check pre-market volume and write whether you'll trade full size or reduced size.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup for today and note whether the overall market supports it.
tasks.why: Matching your size to market conditions reduces unnecessary losses.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades — ticker, entry, exit, size, result.
Trade log. Log each trade and note if you traded the correct size for today's conditions.
Trade log. Write your best entry of the day and why it was clean.
tasks.why: Logging size discipline separates advanced traders from beginners.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for today with a note on size discipline.
Day score. Rate your execution today and write whether you over- or under-sized.
Day score. Write today's score and note one thing you'd change about your sizing.
tasks.why: Scoring size discipline early locks it in as a habit.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the one setup you'll wait for and the level that triggers it.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I will not trade unless price reaches ___' and fill in the number.
Pre-market plan. Write your entry trigger, stop level, and target in one line each.
tasks.why: A three-part plan — entry, stop, target — forces you to think before the open.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trade — entry, stop used, target hit or not.
Trade log. Log whether price hit your target or you exited early and why.
Trade log. Write the result and note if you honored both your stop and your target.
tasks.why: Tracking whether you hit targets reveals if your setups have edge.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note if you let winners run or cut them early.
Day score. Score today and write whether you exited at your planned target.
Day score. Write today's discipline score and note one target management win or failure.
tasks.why: Cutting winners early is as costly as holding losers.

variants: Week check. Write your win rate and net P&L for days 15–19.
Week check. Count wins vs. losses this week and write the ratio.
Week check. Write your best and worst trade from this week with the ticker and amount.
tasks.why: A mid-phase checkpoint shows if your journaling is translating to results.

variants: Rule check. Write the rule you followed best this week.
Rule check. Write the rule you broke most often this week.
Rule check. Write how many trades this week matched your plan vs. how many were impulsive.
tasks.why: Naming what's working and what isn't is how rules improve.

variants: Next week goal. Write the one habit you'll focus on sharpening next week.
Next week goal. Write the number of rule-compliant trades you'll aim for next week.
Next week goal. Write one thing you'll do before every trade next week that you haven't been doing.
tasks.why: A written weekly goal beats a vague intention every time.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's trade idea — ticker, direction, entry trigger.
Pre-market plan. Write the one price level that would make you want to enter a trade today.
Pre-market plan. Write your plan for today's open and the one event that would cancel it.
tasks.why: Starting every day with a written plan is the core habit of this phase.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and mark each one: planned or unplanned.
Trade log. Log your P&L today and write the count of planned vs. unplanned trades.
Trade log. Write each trade ticker and note whether it was in your pre-market plan.
tasks.why: Comparing planned vs. unplanned trades reveals your impulse rate.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for today with a note on planned vs. unplanned ratio.
Day score. Rate today and write whether all your trades were in your pre-market plan.
Day score. Score today and write the number of unplanned trades you took.
tasks.why: Tracking unplanned trades is how you measure impulse control.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the market open conditions — gap up, gap down, or flat — and how that changes your plan.
Pre-market plan. Write whether you'll trade the first 15 minutes or wait and why.
Pre-market plan. Write your plan and note one news event or earnings today that could disrupt it.
tasks.why: Adjusting your plan to today's conditions shows you're reading the market, not just your rules.

variants: Trade log. Write each trade and note if you adjusted for today's conditions.
Trade log. Log today's trades and write whether the open matched your plan.
Trade log. Write today's result and note if gap conditions affected your setup.
tasks.why: Logging how you adapted builds situational flexibility.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note one adjustment you made that helped today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether sitting out during bad conditions was the right call.
Day score. Write today's score and note if adapting your plan improved your result.
tasks.why: Rewarding good adaptations reinforces smart flexibility.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and set a hard stop — the loss amount where you stop for the day.
Pre-market plan. Write today's max loss number before you open your platform.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Today I stop trading if I lose $___' and set a phone alert for it.
tasks.why: Setting your max loss before the session removes the in-the-moment decision.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you stayed under your max loss.
Trade log. Log today's P&L and write whether you hit your daily stop or stayed clear.
Trade log. Write your result and note at what point you would have stopped if it went against you.
tasks.why: Tracking stop compliance is the most important data point in your journal.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether your max loss rule protected you today.
Day score. Rate today and write if you came close to your daily stop — yes or no.
Day score. Score today and write whether pre-committing to a stop made it easier to follow.
tasks.why: Journaling your stop compliance builds the habit one day at a time.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the one setup with the highest win rate from your journal so far.
Pre-market plan. Look at your last 10 trades and write which setup had the best results.
Pre-market plan. Write 'My best setup this month is ___' and only trade that today.
tasks.why: Focusing on your best setup removes lower-probability trades.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you only traded your best setup.
Trade log. Log today's result and write how many trades matched your highest-win-rate setup.
Trade log. Write today's trades and mark each one: best setup or other.
tasks.why: Logging setup type shows how often you drift from your edge.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note how often you stuck to your best setup.
Day score. Rate today and write the one trade where you strayed from your best setup.
Day score. Score today and write whether your results were better when you stuck to the plan.
tasks.why: Connecting setup discipline to results is what builds conviction.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan, then write one scenario where you will NOT trade today.
Pre-market plan. Write the one market condition that would make you sit on your hands today.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I will skip trading today if ___' before the market opens.
tasks.why: Knowing when not to trade is as important as knowing when to trade.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you correctly sat out any bad conditions.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether not trading at any point was the right call.
Trade log. Write your trade count and note if fewer trades led to better results today.
tasks.why: Sitting out bad conditions is an active decision, not a passive one.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note if restraint today saved you from a loss.
Day score. Rate today and write whether you skipped any bad-setup trades.
Day score. Score today and write what you did instead of trading during a bad-conditions window.
tasks.why: Rewarding restraint is how you build patience.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write the news or catalyst you'll watch today and how it affects your setup.
Pre-market plan. Check earnings and news before the open and write whether it changes your plan.
Pre-market plan. Write your trade idea and note if any market-moving news is scheduled today.
tasks.why: Ignoring scheduled news is one of the most avoidable ways to lose money.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if news played a role in the result.
Trade log. Log your trades and write whether any news event invalidated your setup.
Trade log. Write today's result and note if you avoided trading into a news event.
tasks.why: Tracking news impact teaches you when to step aside.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note one time today you used news awareness to your advantage.
Day score. Rate today and write whether news awareness helped or hurt you.
Day score. Score today and write the one news-related decision you're most proud of.
tasks.why: Logging news decisions builds a reference for future events.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your entry, stop, and profit target as three separate lines.
Pre-market plan. Write your risk/reward ratio for today's planned trade.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I risk $__ to make $__' for today's setup and check the ratio.
tasks.why: Planning your R/R before the trade removes emotion from the decision.

variants: Trade log. Write whether today's trade hit your target or you exited early.
Trade log. Log today's result and compare actual R/R to your planned R/R.
Trade log. Write how much you made or lost vs. how much you planned to make or lose.
tasks.why: Comparing actual vs. planned R/R shows where discipline breaks down.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether you hit your planned profit target.
Day score. Rate today and write whether you exited at your target or moved it.
Day score. Score today and write one thing you'd adjust about your target placement.
tasks.why: Tracking target discipline is the second half of stop discipline.

variants: Pre-market plan. Review yesterday's trades and write one adjustment to today's plan.
Pre-market plan. Write how yesterday's result changes your approach today — size, aggression, caution.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Based on yesterday, today I will ___' and make one specific change.
tasks.why: Using yesterday's data to inform today's plan is how pros compound learning.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if yesterday's adjustment helped.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether the change you made from yesterday worked.
Trade log. Write today's trades and compare them to yesterday's — what was different?
tasks.why: Comparing consecutive days shows if your adjustments are working.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note the one adjustment that made the biggest difference today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether yesterday's lesson improved today's execution.
Day score. Score today and write whether you'll carry the same adjustment into tomorrow.
tasks.why: Tracking which adjustments work builds your personal edge.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and rate your confidence in it from 1 to 5 before the open.
Pre-market plan. Write today's setup and note whether you feel sharp or foggy going into the session.
Pre-market plan. Write your plan and add one line about your mental state going into the open.
tasks.why: Tracking mental state before trading shows how much it affects your results.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how your mental state affected your execution.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether your pre-market confidence matched your performance.
Trade log. Write your trades and note if you traded better on days when you felt sharp.
tasks.why: Correlating mental state with results is advanced self-coaching.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note your mental state score vs. your rule-following score.
Day score. Rate today and write whether how you felt predicted how you traded.
Day score. Score today and write one pre-session habit that improves your mental state.
tasks.why: Finding what puts you in a sharp state is a personal performance edge.

variants: Phase 2 review. Write your win rate, net P&L, and avg score from days 15–30.
Phase 2 review. Count your planned vs. unplanned trades from the last two weeks.
Phase 2 review. Write your 3 best and 3 worst trades from days 15–30 with tickers and amounts.
tasks.why: A phase review shows compound progress, not just daily noise.

variants: Rules update. Rewrite any rule that your journal data shows isn't working.
Rules update. Add one rule based on the mistake you made most often in days 15–30.
Rules update. Write the three rules that made you the most money when you followed them.
tasks.why: Updating rules with real data turns the journal into a feedback engine.

variants: Phase 3 goal. Write the win rate you want to hit by day 45.
Phase 3 goal. Write the one discipline habit you'll focus on for the next 15 days.
Phase 3 goal. Write what consistent execution looks like for you over the next two weeks.
tasks.why: Writing a phase goal before starting it increases follow-through.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's setup — ticker, direction, entry trigger.
Pre-market plan. Write the one price level you're watching and what happens if it breaks.
Pre-market plan. Write your trade idea in two sentences and rate your confidence 1–5.
tasks.why: Keeping the pre-market plan habit locked in is the core work of this phase.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades with entry, exit, and result.
Trade log. Log each trade and mark it planned or unplanned.
Trade log. Write today's P&L and total trade count.
tasks.why: Consistent logging is what makes your data useful.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for rule discipline today and one thing you did well.
Day score. Rate today and write the one trade you're most proud of.
Day score. Score today and write one thing you'll sharpen tomorrow.
tasks.why: Daily scoring creates a 90-day improvement record.

variants: Pre-market plan. Check the overall market trend and write whether it supports your setup today.
Pre-market plan. Write whether the market is above or below key levels and how that changes your plan.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup and add one line about broader market conditions.
tasks.why: Trading with the market trend dramatically improves win rate.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if market conditions helped or hurt you.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you traded with or against the market trend.
Trade log. Write your trades and mark each: trend-aligned or counter-trend.
tasks.why: Tracking trend alignment shows one of the biggest drivers of your results.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note how market conditions affected your execution.
Day score. Rate today and write whether trend awareness improved your trades.
Day score. Score today and write one thing you'd change about your market-context read.
tasks.why: Feedback on market-context decisions builds big-picture awareness.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your setup and the one condition that would make you skip today's trade.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I will not trade today if ___' before you open your platform.
Pre-market plan. Write your plan and identify the one chart signal that would invalidate it.
tasks.why: Pre-defining your skip conditions makes staying out of bad trades automatic.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you correctly skipped any bad setups.
Trade log. Log today's result and write how many times you said no to a trade.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one trade you considered but didn't take.
tasks.why: Logging avoided trades shows your skip discipline is improving.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether a skip today saved you money.
Day score. Rate today and write whether fewer trades led to better results.
Day score. Score today and note how your 'no trade' decisions affected your P&L.
tasks.why: Quantifying the value of patience builds conviction in waiting.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and set a profit target — the dollar amount where you stop for the day.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I will stop trading today after making $___' and commit to it.

Setup stats. Write your average win, average loss, and R/R ratio for your focus setup this week.
Setup stats. Write whether your focus setup's win rate this week is above or below your overall average.
tasks.why: Tracking your setup's own stats proves whether it has real edge.

variants: Next week goal. Write one refinement to your focus setup rules based on this week's data.
Next week goal. Write the one condition you'll tighten to improve your focus setup's win rate.
Next week goal. Write 'Next week my focus setup will be stronger because I will ___'.
tasks.why: Refining your setup weekly based on real data is how an edge sharpens.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's focus setup plan and include last week's refinement.
Pre-market plan. Write your updated setup conditions after last week's review.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Based on last week I changed ___ in my setup and today I'll test it'.
tasks.why: Testing a refinement the day after making it closes the feedback loop fast.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how the refinement performed.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether the updated conditions improved entry quality.
Trade log. Write your trades and note if the refinement changed when you entered vs. last week.
tasks.why: Testing refinements in the market is the only way to know if they work.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note the refinement's effect on today's execution.
Day score. Rate today and write whether the update improved your setup's performance.
Day score. Score today and write whether you'll keep the refinement or adjust it.
tasks.why: Fast iteration on refinements is how your setup evolves from good to great.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup plan and add your exit strategy in one line.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I exit when ___' for both winners and losers for today's trade.
Pre-market plan. Write your entry trigger, stop, and two exit scenarios — one for a winner, one for a loser.
tasks.why: Planning both exit scenarios before the trade removes emotion from the exit decision.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how each exit matched your pre-plan.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you exited winners and losers at the right points.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one exit you're most and least happy with.
tasks.why: Grading exits separately from entries reveals a hidden source of edge.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for exit quality today alongside your entry quality score.
Day score. Rate today and write whether your exits improved vs. last week.
Day score. Score today and write the one exit adjustment that would improve your P&L most.
tasks.why: Tracking exit quality separately accelerates improvement in the hardest trading skill.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup plan and flag the one risk that could hurt you today.
Pre-market plan. Write the one thing that could go wrong with today's trade and how you'll handle it.
Pre-market plan. Write 'The biggest risk to today's trade is ___ and I'll respond by ___'.
tasks.why: Pre-naming risks forces you to prepare responses instead of react.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note whether the risk you flagged appeared.
Trade log. Log today's result and write how you handled any adverse move.
Trade log. Write your trades and note whether your pre-planned risk response worked.
tasks.why: Tracking how prepared responses hold up in real time calibrates your pre-planning.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note how well you managed today's flagged risk.
Day score. Rate today and write whether pre-naming the risk improved your response.
Day score. Score today and write one risk you didn't anticipate that you'll flag next time.
tasks.why: Building a risk vocabulary for your setup protects you from surprises.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup and how today's price action confirms or denies it.
Pre-market plan. Write the specific price action signal you'll wait for before triggering your setup.
Pre-market plan. Write your plan and add a confirmation signal — the last thing you need to see before entry.
tasks.why: Adding a confirmation signal reduces false entries in your focus setup.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if each entry had a confirmation signal.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether trades with confirmations outperformed those without.
Trade log. Write your trades and mark each one: confirmed entry or anticipated entry.
tasks.why: Comparing confirmed vs. anticipated entries reveals how much confirmation matters for your setup.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note what percentage of entries had a confirmation signal.
Day score. Rate today and write whether requiring confirmation improved your win rate.
Day score. Score today and write the confirmation signal that works best for your focus setup.
tasks.why: Identifying your best confirmation signal sharpens your edge in one step.

variants: Weekly review. Write your focus setup win rate, net P&L, and avg score for the past 5 days.
Weekly review. Write the setup details for your 3 best executions this week.
Weekly review. Write how many focus setup opportunities appeared vs. how many you took this week.
tasks.why: Week 2 of single-setup focus starts showing whether the specialization is paying off.

variants: Cumulative stats. Write your focus setup's win rate across all of phase 3 so far.
Cumulative stats. Write your R/R ratio for your focus setup over the last 10 days.
Cumulative stats. Write your focus setup's average win and average loss over the last 2 weeks.
tasks.why: Cumulative stats on one setup reveal its true edge — or lack of one.

variants: Refinement. Write one adjustment to your focus setup's entry rules based on the last 10 days.
Refinement. Write the confirmation signal you'll add or remove from your focus setup next week.
Refinement. Write 'My focus setup's win rate would improve if I changed ___'.
tasks.why: Refining your setup on a weekly cadence is how it compounds into a real edge.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup plan and apply this week's refinement.
Pre-market plan. Write the updated entry conditions for your focus setup after last week's review.
Pre-market plan. Write 'This week my setup is sharper because I changed ___'.
tasks.why: Each week of refinement adds another layer of precision to your edge.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how the refinement felt in real time.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether the updated conditions improved entry quality.
Trade log. Write your trades and compare the quality of entries to last week's.
tasks.why: Testing refinements back-to-back weeks reveals if each change is additive.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note the refinement's effect on today's execution.
Day score. Rate today and write whether you're more confident in your setup than two weeks ago.
Day score. Score today and write the word that best describes your setup confidence right now.
tasks.why: Tracking confidence in your setup over time shows compounding conviction.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and the one mistake you've been most tempted to make this week.
Pre-market plan. Write the one rule you've been closest to breaking in the last 5 sessions.
Pre-market plan. Write 'The rule I most need to hold today is ___' at the top of your plan.
tasks.why: Naming the rule under pressure before the session keeps it front of mind.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note whether the rule you flagged held.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether the pressure rule showed up — and what you did.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one moment you felt pulled to break your flagged rule.
tasks.why: Tracking how you handle pressure rules reveals your real discipline level.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether your flagged rule held under pressure today.
Day score. Rate today and write what helped you hold your hardest rule.
Day score. Score today and write whether pre-naming the rule made it easier to follow.
tasks.why: Holding your hardest rule consistently is what separates good traders from great ones.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup plan and note how many times it has appeared this month.
Pre-market plan. Write the frequency of your focus setup in the last 4 weeks — roughly how many per day.
Pre-market plan. Write whether your focus setup is high-frequency or low-frequency and how that shapes your patience.
tasks.why: Knowing your setup's frequency helps you calibrate patience and avoid forcing trades.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how long you waited for your focus setup to appear.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you took any trade while waiting for your setup.
Trade log. Write your trades and note how many valid setups appeared vs. how many you took.
tasks.why: Tracking wait time shows whether patience is improving over the phase.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note how patient you were waiting for your setup today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether you filled idle time with bad trades or did nothing.
Day score. Score today and write one thing you do while waiting for a setup that keeps you sharp.
tasks.why: Active patience is a skill — not just the absence of trading.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and identify the three elements of your focus setup in one line each: entry, stop, target.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup from memory — without looking at your rules doc — as a test.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup rules as if explaining them to a friend in under 5 sentences.
tasks.why: If you can't write your setup from memory, you don't know it well enough to trade it under pressure.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you needed to check your rules mid-session.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether your setup rules felt automatic or still conscious.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one rule that still requires active thought.
tasks.why: Rules that require active thought under pressure are not yet internalized.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note which rules feel automatic and which still need attention.
Day score. Rate today and write the one rule you could recite in your sleep.
Day score. Score today and write the one rule that still doesn't feel natural yet.
tasks.why: Internalized rules execute under pressure — rules that aren't can't.

variants: Weekly review. Write your focus setup win rate, net P&L, and avg score for the past 5 days.
Weekly review. Write the number of focus setup trades this week and their combined P&L.
Weekly review. Write the 3 best and 2 worst setups from this week with the key lesson from each.
tasks.why: Week 3 of single-setup focus is when the data starts showing a reliable pattern.

variants: Phase 3 check. Write your cumulative win rate for your focus setup across days 61–75.
Phase 3 check. Write whether your focus setup's win rate has improved week over week in phase 3.
Phase 3 check. Write your setup's R/R and win rate for all of phase 3 and note the trend.
tasks.why: Fifteen days of single-setup data tells you definitively if the edge is real.

variants: Final phase prep. Write the one refinement you'll make to your setup for days 76–90.
Final phase prep. Write 'My setup is ready for the final 15 days because ___'.
Final phase prep. Write the one thing that would make your setup execution perfect by day 90.
tasks.why: Entering the final phase with a clear intention sharpens the last stretch.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your focus setup plan with the final-phase refinement at the top.
Pre-market plan. Write your updated setup rules in their cleanest, most concise form.
Pre-market plan. Write your trade idea and rate how confident you are in your setup on a 1–5 scale.
tasks.why: Starting the final phase with your sharpest rules is the goal of everything before this.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and grade your execution on the final-phase standard.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you traded your setup at the highest level you've achieved.
Trade log. Write your trades and note one thing you executed better today than on day 61.
tasks.why: Comparing your execution to your own best is the right benchmark at this stage.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 for today and note one thing that feels noticeably better than day 61.
Day score. Rate today and write the single most improved skill since you started this phase.
Day score. Score today and write what trading this setup feels like now vs. day 1.
tasks.why: Acknowledging real improvement at the start of the final phase builds momentum.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's focus setup plan and the one condition that would make you skip it.
Pre-market plan. Write whether today's market environment is ideal, acceptable, or poor for your setup.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Today is a ___ environment for my setup and I will trade ___ size'.
tasks.why: Calibrating your plan to market quality is a mark of a mature trader.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how the environment affected your setup's performance.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether environment awareness changed any decisions.
Trade log. Write your trades and note one market condition cue you noticed today that you would have missed on day 1.
tasks.why: Noticing what you used to miss is a measure of expertise developing.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note how well you read the market environment today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether environment awareness saved you from a bad trade.
Day score. Score today and write the one market condition cue that is most predictive for your setup.
tasks.why: Knowing your setup's ideal environment is the deepest form of setup mastery.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and your max daily loss for today — set it before you open your platform.
Pre-market plan. Write both your max loss and your daily profit target before the session.
Pre-market plan. Write 'I stop at -$___ and I stop at +$___' to bookend today's session.
tasks.why: Bookending your session with both a stop and a target gives the day a shape.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if you stayed within your bookends.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you stopped at the right time — whether positive or negative.
Trade log. Write your P&L and note whether you hit your target and stopped or kept going.
tasks.why: Honoring both bookends is the final piece of session management.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether you honored both bookends today.
Day score. Rate today and write which bookend — stop or target — is harder to respect.
Day score. Score today and write the bookend rule you'll commit to perfecting this week.
tasks.why: Perfect bookend discipline is the goal of the final phase.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's plan and note whether you're in a drawdown, breakeven, or profit period.
Pre-market plan. Write your running P&L for this week and how it affects today's sizing.
Pre-market plan. Write 'This week I am +/- $___' and decide if that changes your plan for today.
tasks.why: Knowing your running P&L before each session keeps you from making size decisions emotionally.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note if your week's P&L context affected your execution.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether being in a drawdown or profit changed how you traded.
Trade log. Write your trades and note if you traded bigger or smaller based on your running week P&L.
tasks.why: Tracking the interaction between running P&L and sizing shows if emotions affect your risk.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether your running P&L influenced your discipline today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether you traded differently because of your week's results.
Day score. Score today and write the rule that keeps your sizing consistent regardless of P&L.
tasks.why: Keeping sizing consistent through P&L swings is the mark of a professional.

variants: Weekly review. Write your focus setup win rate, net P&L, and avg score for the past 5 days.
Weekly review. Write your 3 best executions this week and the one thing each had in common.
Weekly review. Write your setup stats for the week: trades taken, win rate, avg win, avg loss.
tasks.why: Week 4 of single-setup focus is the deepest you've gone — the data is now meaningful.

variants: 90-day progress. Write your win rate and avg score from day 1 vs. today.
90-day progress. Write the 3 biggest improvements in your trading since day 1.
90-day progress. Write the one habit that has had the most positive impact on your results.
tasks.why: Seeing your progress from day 1 is the most motivating data point in the entire program.

variants: Final 10 days. Write what you want to be true about your trading by day 90.
Final 10 days. Write 'By day 90 I will have ___' as a specific trading achievement.
Final 10 days. Write the one thing you want to lock in before the 90 days are up.
tasks.why: A clear finish-line goal in the final stretch turns intention into execution.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's plan and your finish-line goal from yesterday at the top.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup and one action today that moves you toward your day-90 goal.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Today I'll move closer to my day-90 goal by ___'.
tasks.why: Connecting each day in the final stretch to your finish-line goal keeps momentum high.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note one execution detail you've mastered since day 1.
Trade log. Log today's result and write one moment where your skill level was visibly higher than before.
Trade log. Write your trades and note whether you're executing your setup at your own highest level.
tasks.why: Measuring against your own best is the right benchmark in the final phase.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and compare to your average score from phase 1.
Day score. Rate today and write whether your scores in the final phase are your career best.
Day score. Score today and write the one skill that is undeniably stronger than day 1.
tasks.why: The delta from day 1 to day 81 is the measure of what this program built.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write your plan and identify the one remaining weakness you want to eliminate before day 90.
Pre-market plan. Write the one rule you still break occasionally and commit to perfecting it this week.
Pre-market plan. Write 'Before day 90 I will fix ___' and build today's plan around it.
tasks.why: Naming one final weakness to eliminate is the most productive use of the last 8 days.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note how the final weakness showed up or didn't.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether you held your targeted rule today.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one moment where your final weakness was tested.
tasks.why: Tracking a single final weakness over the last 8 days can eliminate it permanently.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether your final weakness appeared today.
Day score. Rate today and write whether this weakness is shrinking.
Day score. Score today and write what eliminating this weakness would mean for your results.

variants: Pre-market plan. Write today's plan and your current 7-day win rate.
Pre-market plan. Write your results for the last 7 days and whether the trend is positive.
Pre-market plan. Write 'My last 7 days show ___ and today I will ___' based on that data.

Trade log. Log today's result and write whether today improved or hurt your 7-day streak.
Trade log. Write your trades and note whether you're trading your best in this final stretch.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note whether today's score raised or lowered your 7-day average.
Day score. Rate today and write whether the final stretch is bringing out your best.
Day score. Score today and write what you're doing better in the final 8 days than at any prior point.
tasks.why: Finishing with your best numbers creates momentum that carries beyond day 90.

Pre-market plan. Write every condition, trigger, stop, and target rule for your focus setup without looking at your notes.
Pre-market plan. Write your setup rules as a clean, numbered checklist — from memory.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note which rules you applied without thinking.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether any rule required conscious effort to follow.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the one rule that is now fully automatic.
tasks.why: Automatic rules execute under pressure — that is the goal of 84 days of practice.

variants: Day score. Write 1–5 and note which rules feel fully automatic today.
Day score. Rate today and write the rule that took the longest to internalize.
Day score. Score today and write what trading feels like now that most rules are automatic.
tasks.why: Automatic execution is the final form of rule-based trading.

variants: Weekly review. Write your focus setup win rate, net P&L, and avg score for the past 5 days.
Weekly review. Write the 3 executions this week you're most proud of and why.
Weekly review. Write your best and worst trade from the week and the one lesson from each.

variants: Final-phase stats. Write your focus setup's cumulative win rate, R/R, and avg win from days 76–85.

Final-phase stats. Write the three metrics that improved the most since you started single-setup focus.

Final 5 days. Write the one thing you'll execute perfectly in each of the last 5 days.
Final 5 days. Write 'For the final 5 days I will ___' as a specific commitment.

Trade log. Write your trades and note one thing that made today's execution better than 84 days ago.

Day score. Score today and write one thing you'll do tomorrow to match or beat today.

Pre-market plan. Write your plan using only your highest-conviction version of your focus setup.

Trade log. Write your trades and note how selective you were about which signals you took.

Day score. Score today and write whether 87 days of practice has changed how you see setups.

Pre-market plan. Write today's plan as cleanly as you've ever written it — three lines: setup, stop, target.

variants: Trade log. Write today's trades and note whether the execution matched the plan precision.
Trade log. Log today's result and write whether bookend discipline held.
Trade log. Write your trades and note one execution detail that would have been impossible on day 1.
tasks.why: Concrete execution improvements prove the work was real.

Day score. Rate today and write what the last two days need to look like to close strong.
Day score. Score today and write the two most important things to carry beyond day 90.

Pre-market plan. Write today's setup and note what a perfect final two sessions looks like.

Trade log. Log today's result and write whether today's execution reflects 89 days of work.
Trade log. Write your trades and note the single best execution decision you made today.

Day score. Rate today and write one thing you're carrying into day 90.
Day score. Score today and write what you want tomorrow to look like.

90-day review. Write your win rate from day 1–14 vs. days 76–90 and the difference.
90-day review. Write the 3 rules that made you the most money when you followed them.

variants: What's next. Write the one thing you'll keep doing after day 90, every single session.
